name: Hybrid Multi-Vendor (Microsoft + Netskope + CrowdStrike)
description: The most comprehensive stack: Microsoft Sentinel and Purview for SIEM and DLP, Netskope as SWG/CASB, and CrowdStrike Falcon on the endpoint. Common in large enterprises that have best-of-breed tooling across security domains. Provides the deepest detection coverage across all platforms.

things_to_know.title: Overlapping detection is a feature, not a bug
things_to_know.content: Multiple tools will detect the same AI activity. Use your SIEM to deduplicate and correlate. The overlap provides defense-in-depth: if one tool misses, another catches it.

things_to_know.title: Deployment sequence matters
things_to_know.content: Deploy detection in phases: identity and network first (Conditional Access, generic rules), then CASB/SWG (Netskope, MCAS), then endpoint (CrowdStrike, Defender), then DLP. This prevents alert fatigue from deploying everything simultaneously.

things_to_know.title: Sentinel as the correlation hub
things_to_know.content: Use Microsoft Sentinel as the central correlation engine. Ingest CrowdStrike and Netskope events via data connectors. All runbook triggers should fire from Sentinel analytics rules for a single pane of glass.

things_to_know.title: License cost is significant
things_to_know.content: This stack requires licenses for Microsoft E5 (or equivalent), Netskope Intelligent SSE, and CrowdStrike Falcon. Ensure budget approval before committing to full deployment.

policy_token_replacements.siem_tool: Microsoft Sentinel (with CrowdStrike and Netskope data connectors)
policy_token_replacements.dlp_tool: Microsoft Purview Endpoint DLP + Netskope inline DLP + CrowdStrike Falcon Data Protection
policy_token_replacements.edr_tool: CrowdStrike Falcon Insight (primary) + Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (secondary)
policy_token_replacements.casb_tool: Netskope CASB + Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps
